user_message: Summarize what happened with Wide World Importers and give me the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-27 09:05 EST
Subject: Follow-up: territory planning Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about territory planning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
Date: 2024-01-14 11:48 EST
Subject: URGENT: territory planning Issue at Wide World Importers

Our team encountered some challenges with territory planning. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: territory planning Discussion - Wide World Importers
Date: 2024-02-21
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is territory planning.

[00:07:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:12:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on integration complexity and found that there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us.

[00:15:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:17:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:20:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for timeline constraints is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
